question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the complete list of all hook events a Juju charm can observe:

---

## 🔁 Lifecycle Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `install` | Fires once before any other hook, when the unit is first provisioned. |
| `config-changed` | Fires after `install`, after `leader-elected`, after `upgrade-charm`, and on any config/trust change or agent restart. |
| `start` | Fires after the first `config-changed`. Signals initialization is complete. |
| `update-status` | Fired periodically by Juju (default: every 5 min) to poll health and update status. |
| `upgrade-charm` | Fires once immediately after a `juju refresh` (charm code update). |
| `stop` | Fires when Juju is about to destroy the unit. Should gracefully stop services. |
| `remove` | Fires once as the very last hook before the unit is fully destroyed. |

---

## 👑 Leadership Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | Fires on the unit that becomes the application leader. |
| `leader-settings-changed` | Fires on all **non-leader** units when leadership changes or the leader calls `leader-set`. |

---

## 🔗 Relation Hooks *(prefixed with endpoint name)*

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<endpoint>-relation-created` | Fires when an application joins a new relation (before any remote units are seen). |
| `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | Fires once when a new remote unit first appears in the relation. |
| `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | Fires after `relation-joined` and whenever a related unit updates its relation data. |
| `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | Fires when a related unit leaves the relation. |
| `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | Fires when the entire relation is removed (inverse of `relation-created`). |

---

## 💾 Storage Hooks *(prefixed with storage name)*

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | Fires when a storage volume is attached and ready. Runs before `install` on machine charms. |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | Fires after `stop` and before `remove` when storage is being detached. |

---

## 🐳 Workload / Pebble Hooks *(Kubernetes sidecar charms only, prefixed with container name)*

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | Fires when the workload container is up and the Pebble API is accessible. |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | Fires when a Pebble `custom` notice is recorded in the workload container. |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | Fires when a Pebble health check crosses its failure threshold. *(Juju 3.6+)* |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | Fires when a previously-failed Pebble health check passes again. *(Juju 3.6+)* |

---

## 🔐 Secret Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | Fires on a **secret consumer** when the owner publishes a new revision. |
| `secret-rotate` | Fires on the **secret owner** when the rotation interval elapses and a new revision should be created. |
| `secret-expired` | Fires on the **secret owner** when a revision's expiry time is reached. |
| `secret-remove` | Fires on the **secret owner** when a revision has no more consumers and can be removed. |

---

## 🔼 Upgrade Series Hooks *(machine charms only)*

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Fires when `juju upgrade-series <machine> prepare` is run. Charm prepares before OS upgrade. |
| `post-series-upgrade` | Fires after the OS series upgrade completes. Charm recovers/reconfigures. |

---

## ⚡ Action Hooks

| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<action-name>` | One hook per action defined in `actions.yaml`, fired when an operator runs `juju run <unit> <action>`. |

---

## 🧩 Ops Framework Synthetic Events *(not Juju hooks — synthesized by the `ops` library)*

| Event | Description |
|---|---|
| `collect-unit-status` | Fired at the end of every hook. Charms report unit status via `event.add_status()`. |
| `collect-app-status` | Same as above but for **application status**; only fires on the **leader unit**. |

---

## ❌ Deprecated / Removed

| Hook | Status |
|---|---|
| `collect-metrics` | **Removed** in Juju 3.6 |
| `meter-status-changed` | **Removed** alongside the metrics infrastructure |

---

**Key takeaways:**
- Relation, storage, workload, and action hooks are all **name-prefixed** based on what you define in `metadata.yaml` / `actions.yaml`.
- Pebble hooks only apply to **Kubernetes sidecar charms**.
- `collect-unit-status` / `collect-app-status` are `ops`-library constructs, not physical Juju hook files.
